free_text: Light nuclei emitted in the fission of 252Cf
Cf-252 sample with about 4.E7 spontaneous fissions per
minute, on a thin nickel foil. This foil was then
placed on a thick aluminum backing, and a cover foil of
450 mu-g/cm2 of nickel was placed over the 252Cf.
For the light particles a simple
two-counter telescope was used: the E detector was a
lithium-drifted silicon device with a thickness of 2
mm, the deltaE detector was a surface-barrier type,
having a totally depleted thickness of 62 mu-m. For
the heavier particles a diffused-junction detector of
either 12 or 14 mu-m, was used. The deltaE detector
was 3.5 cm from the source, and collimators
restricted the acceptance angle of the telescope
system to about 7 degrees. Fission fragments were
detectors. The fission detector was collimated to an
acceptance angle of 20 degrees, or in a few runs, 30
